title: Cardiologists
description: Diagnose, treat, manage, and prevent diseases or conditions of the cardiovascular system. May further subspecialize in interventional procedures (e.g., balloon angioplasty and stent placement), echocardiography, or electrophysiology.

tasks.text: Administer emergency cardiac care for life-threatening heart problems, such as cardiac arrest and heart attack.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Advise patients and community members concerning diet, activity, hygiene, or disease prevention.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Answer questions that patients have about their health and well-being.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Calculate valve areas from blood flow velocity measurements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compare measurements of heart wall thickness and chamber sizes to standards to identify abnormalities, using the results of an echocardiogram.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct electrocardiogram (EKG), phonocardiogram, echocardiogram, or other cardiovascular tests to record patients' cardiac activity, using specialized electronic test equipment, recording devices, or laboratory instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct exercise electrocardiogram tests to monitor cardiovascular activity under stress.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Conduct research to develop or test medications, treatments, or procedures that prevent or control disease or injury.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct tests of the pulmonary system, using a spirometer or other respiratory testing equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design and explain treatment plans, based on patient information such as medical history, reports, and examination results.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Diagnose cardiovascular conditions, using cardiac catheterization.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Diagnose medical conditions of patients, using records, reports, test results, or examination information.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Explain procedures and discuss test results or prescribed treatments with patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inject contrast media into patients' blood vessels.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor patients' conditions and progress, and reevaluate treatments, as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Observe ultrasound display screen, and listen to signals to record vascular information, such as blood pressure, limb volume changes, oxygen saturation, and cerebral circulation.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Obtain and record patient information, including patient identification, medical history, and examination results.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate diagnostic imaging equipment to produce contrast-enhanced radiographs of heart and cardiovascular system.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Order medical tests, such as echocardiograms, electrocardiograms, and angiograms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform minimally invasive surgical procedures, such as implanting pacemakers and defibrillators.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform vascular procedures, such as balloon angioplasty and stents.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prescribe heart medication to treat or prevent heart problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend surgeons or surgical procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise or train cardiology technologists or students.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Talk to other physicians about patients to create a treatment plan.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
